items.title: Pandas GroupBy & Joins
items.description: Customer-level aggregates, rolling counts, and merge-asof joins are the pandas mechanics behind many derived history features created before modeling.

items.title: Feature extraction
items.description: Feature creation adds hand-designed columns from domain rules; feature extraction learns automatic mappings (vectorizers, encoders) via sklearn Transformers with fit/transform.

items.title: Linear regression
items.description: Linear models are additive unless you supply interaction and derived columns explicitly—creation work directly shapes what linear regression can represent.

items.title: Decision trees
items.description: Trees can approximate interactions via sequential splits, but explicit domain interactions and datetime flags still improve interpretability and can reduce depth needed.

items.title: Cleaning and transformation
items.description: Imputation, type casting, and outlier handling should precede ratios and datetime parsing so created features operate on consistent, trustworthy inputs.
